title: Percents Greater Than 100% and Less Than 1%
contentObjective: I can write and interpret percents greater than 100% and less than 1%.
languageObjective: I can explain my reasoning using the words percent, greater than 100%, less than 1%, and equivalent.

vocabulary.definition: A way to compare a number to 100, shown with the % sign.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una manera de comparar un número con 100, con el signo %.
vocabulary.cloze: A number out of 100, written with a % sign, is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A percent bigger than 100%, more than the whole thing.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un porcentaje mayor que 100%, más que todo el entero.
vocabulary.cloze: A percent that represents more than one whole, like 150%, is ___.

vocabulary.definition: A percent smaller than 1%, a very tiny part.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un porcentaje menor que 1%, una parte muy pequeña.
vocabulary.visual: 0.5% of 1,000 = only 5 items — a tiny sliver
vocabulary.cloze: A percent smaller than one out of a hundred, like 0.5%, is ___.

vocabulary.definition: Having the same value, just written a different way.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Tener el mismo valor, escrito de otra forma.
vocabulary.cloze: Values that name the same amount, like 0.5% and 0.005, are ___.

vocabulary.definition: A whole number plus a fraction, like 1 1/2.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número entero más una fracción, como 1 1/2.
vocabulary.cloze: A number with a whole part and a fraction part, like 1 1/2, is a ___.

launch.narrative: The arcade just posted its monthly leaderboard! Some players crushed the target score — one player hit 150% of the goal! Meanwhile, a rare bonus round gives only a 0.5% point boost. You need to understand what these unusual percents really mean to read the leaderboard correctly.
launch.contextImage: Arcade leaderboard screen showing scores with percents above 100% and below 1%
noticePrompts: What does it mean when a score is shown as 150% of the target?
Can a percent be less than 1%? What would that look like?
wonderPrompts: How do you write a percent greater than 100% as a decimal or fraction?
When would you see a percent less than 1% in real life?

explore.instructions: Sort each value into the correct category: Greater than 100%, Between 1% and 100%, or Less than 1%.

discourse.prompt: What do you notice about the decimal form of percents in each category?
discourse.sentenceFrame: Percents greater than 100% have a decimal value ___ than 1, while percents less than 1% have a decimal value ___.

optional.stem: What is 150% written as a decimal?
What is 0.5% written as a decimal?
A price rises to 120% of its original value. The original was $40. What is the new price?

approaching.stem: Which of the following is greater than 100%?
approaching.explanation: 1.25 = 125%, which is greater than 100%. The other values are all less than 1 (less than 100%).

approaching.stem: What is 200% written as a decimal?
approaching.explanation: 200% ÷ 100 = 2.0. Percents greater than 100% become decimals greater than 1.

approaching.stem: What is 0.5% written as a decimal?
approaching.explanation: 0.5% ÷ 100 = 0.005. Move the decimal two places to the left.
0.75% is less than 1%. All the other choices are greater than 1%.

approaching.instructions: Sort each value: is the percent greater than 100%, between 1% and 100%, or less than 1%?

onLevel.instructions: Match each value to its equivalent percent.

onLevel.stem: A player scored 0.4% of the bonus points. Which decimal represents this percent?
onLevel.explanation: 0.4% = 0.4 ÷ 100 = 0.004. Move the decimal point two places to the left.

onLevel.instructions: Convert each percent to a decimal and a fraction.

onLevel.label: Place these percents on a number line from 0 to 3: A = 50%, B = 150%, C = 275%

extending.title: Find the Percent Error

workedExample.label: Move the decimal two places left

extending.correctWork: 0.6% means 0.6 out of 100. Move the decimal two places left: 0.6 → 0.006. The correct answer is 0.6% = 0.006.
hints: The student only moved the decimal one place instead of two.
0.6% is less than 1%, so the decimal must be less than 0.01. 0.06 is too large.

extending.prompt: A game character has 300% of its original health after picking up a power-up. If the original health was 80 points, how many health points does the character have now? A student says 240 points. Another says 380 points. Who is correct and why?
extending.sentenceFrame: The student who said ___ is correct because 300% means ___ times the original, so ___ × 80 = ___.

extending.instructions: A bacteria population starts at 500. Complete the table showing what each percent of the original population equals.

connect.scenario: An arcade machine has a jackpot that pays out 250% of your tokens if you win. You inserted 40 tokens. Your friend says the jackpot is 100 tokens. Another friend says it's 60 tokens.
connect.promptQuestion: Who is correct, and how many tokens does the jackpot pay?
connect.prompt: The friend who said ___ tokens is correct because 250% of 40 = ___ × 40 = ___. The other friend found ___% of 40 instead.

exitTicket.explanation: 0.25% = 0.25 ÷ 100 = 0.0025. It is much less than 1%, not equal to 1/4.
